codeSystems.name: PBSItemCodes
codeSystems.title: PBS Item Codes
codeSystems.description: This CodeSystem resource represents the items listed on the Australian Pharmaceutical Benefits Scheme (PBS) schedule published by the Department of Health. Codes are represented as five or six characters in format (n)nnnna and the display is the PBS prescribing rule preferred term. This is not the canonical publication of this code system nor it is intended to be publication on behalf of the Department of Health. For the complete content of the code system contact the publisher.

codeSystems.name: AustralianDigitalHealthSourceSystem
codeSystems.title: Australian Digital Health Source System
codeSystems.description: The Australian Digital Health Source System code system defines concepts that represent Australian digital health sources. These concepts may be used to represent which system clinical and/or administrative information is sourced.

codeSystems.name: CommunicationRequestCategory
codeSystems.title: Communication Request Category
codeSystems.description: The Communication Request Category code system defines concepts that represent the category for a communication request.

codeSystems.name: LocationTypePhysicalAU
codeSystems.title: Location Type (Physical) AU
codeSystems.description: Additional concept codes for location physical type defined for use in Australian context. These codes are used as extensions to the HL7 International code set.

codeSystems.name: LocationType
codeSystems.title: Location Type AU
codeSystems.description: Additional concept codes for location type defined for use in an Australian context. These codes are used as extensions to the HL7 International code set.

codeSystems.name: MedicationType
codeSystems.title: Medication Type
codeSystems.description: The Medication Type code system defines concepts in a classification scheme for medication coding. The classification scheme defines the nature of the properties defined by the code e.g. branded product with no strength or form. This allows the distinction between codes provided containing container, pack, strength and form explicitly especially where terminologies supplied are unknown or terminology services are unavailable.

codeSystems.name: MedicineItemChange
codeSystems.title: Medicine Item Change
codeSystems.description: The Medicine Item Change code system defines concepts that identify a change that has been made, or is recommended to be made, to a medicine item.

codeSystems.name: NameContext
codeSystems.title: Name Context
codeSystems.description: The Name Context code system defines concepts describing usage of names. This provides well known concepts that can be used to indicate usage to achieve an associated purpose e.g. identifier validation.

codeSystems.name: ResourceTag
codeSystems.title: Resource Tag
codeSystems.description: The Resource Tag code system defines concepts that represent tag values for labelling resources.

codeSystems.name: AURecordedSexOrGenderSourceDocumentType
codeSystems.title: AU Recorded Sex or Gender Source Document Type
codeSystems.description: The AU Recorded Sex or Gender Source Document Type code system defines concepts that represent the type of document where this sex or gender value is recorded.

codeSystems.name: AURecordedSexOrGenderType
codeSystems.title: AU Recorded Sex or Gender Type
codeSystems.description: The AU Recorded Sex or Gender Type code system defines concepts that represent the type or category of sex or gender that is recorded.

codeSystems.name: ServiceProvisionConditionsAustralianConcepts
codeSystems.title: Service Provision Conditions Australian Concepts
codeSystems.description: The Service Provision Conditions Australian Concepts code system defines concepts that identify the conditions under which a healthcare service is available/offered.

codeSystems.name: TaskBusinessStatus
codeSystems.title: Task Business Status
codeSystems.description: The Task Business Status code system defines concepts that represent business status values for a task.

codeSystems.name: IdentifierTypeAU
codeSystems.title: IdentifierType AU
codeSystems.description: Additional concept codes for identifier type defined for use in an Australian context. These are additional identifier type codes to the HL7 International code set and suitable for use with Australian identifiers and may be used in HL7 V2.x identifier representations.

codeSystems.name: DegreeLicenseCertificateAU
codeSystems.title: DegreeLicenseCertificate AU
codeSystems.description: Additional concept codes for qualification defined for use in an Australian context. These codes are used as extensions to the HL7 International code set.

codeSystems.name: ProviderRoleAU
codeSystems.title: providerRole AU
codeSystems.description: Additional concept codes for specifying the functional involvement with the activity being transmitted (e.g., Case Manager, Evaluator, Transcriber, Nurse Care Practitioner, Midwife, Physician Assistant, etc.) defined for use in an Australian context. These codes are used as extensions to the HL7 International code set.

codeSystems.name: ActCodeAU
codeSystems.title: ActCode AU
codeSystems.description: Additional concept codes for act coding defined for use in an Australian context. These codes are used as extensions to the HL7 International code set.

codeSystems.name: MIMS
codeSystems.title: Monthly Index of Medical Specialties (MIMS)
codeSystems.description: This CodeSystem resource represents the Monthly Index of Medical Specialties (MIMS) code system published by MIMS Australia. The intended use of MIMS data is to fully specify the product as a combination of product code, form code, and pack code. For example, when providing a MIMS Code within a Clinical Document Architecture (CDA) document, the MIMS code will be a minimum of 5 digits (in most cases, will be 8 digits).This is not the canonical publication of this code system nor it is intended to be publication on behalf of the MIMS Australia. For the complete content of the code system contact the publisher.
